seoTitle: How to Set Up Lead Scoring in HubSpot (2026)
metaDescription: Build a HubSpot lead scoring model from scratch: define Fit vs. Engagement criteria, assign point values, and set threshold tags to flag hot prospects.
intro: Not every lead deserves the same amount of sales attention, and HubSpot's Lead Scoring tool lets you quantify that difference. This walkthrough covers building a scoring model that separates demographic fit from behavioral engagement, so your team can prioritize the right contacts.

steps.heading: Open the Lead Scoring builder
steps.body: From your HubSpot Marketing dashboard, navigate to the Lead Scoring workspace and start a new scoring model.

steps.heading: Choose your target object and model type
steps.body: Decide whether you're scoring Contacts, Companies, or Deals, and set up separate score types for Fit and Engagement.

steps.heading: Set property-based Fit rules
steps.body: Build rule rows that award positive points for ideal demographic or firmographic properties, such as job title or company size.

steps.heading: Add Engagement criteria
steps.body: Create a separate rule group that scores behavioral signals like page views, form submissions, and email clicks.

steps.heading: Set score thresholds and test it
steps.body: Define color-coded categories for score ranges to flag hot versus cold leads, run a quick audit against an existing contact, and publish the model live.

faq.question: What's the difference between Fit score and Engagement score in HubSpot?
faq.answer: Fit score measures how closely a contact matches your ideal customer profile based on properties like job title or industry, while Engagement score tracks how actively they interact with your content.

faq.question: Can I score companies and deals, not just contacts?
faq.answer: Yes, HubSpot's Lead Scoring tool lets you build separate models targeting Contacts, Companies, or Deals depending on your sales process.

faq.question: Will lead scoring changes apply to existing contacts automatically?
faq.answer: Once you publish the model, HubSpot recalculates scores for existing records based on their current property values and activity history.

faq.question: Is lead scoring available on HubSpot's free plan?
faq.answer: Lead Scoring is generally a feature available on paid Marketing Hub tiers rather than the free CRM, so check your subscription level before setting it up.
